name: Andrej Karpathy's Reading List
description: Essential ML papers curated by Andrej Karpathy. Focuses on language models, GPT series, retrieval-augmented generation, alignment, and analysis.

papers.title: Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models
papers.abstract: Empirical study showing power-law relationships between model performance and size, data, and compute.

papers.title: Language Models are Unsupervised Multitask Learners
papers.abstract: GPT-2 paper showing that language models can perform many tasks zero-shot through next-token prediction.

papers.title: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners
papers.abstract: GPT-3 paper demonstrating emergent few-shot learning capabilities at scale.

papers.title: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
papers.abstract: InstructGPT/RLHF paper showing how to align language models with human intent.

papers.title: Visual Instruction Tuning (LLaVA)
papers.abstract: Large Language and Vision Assistant combining vision encoders with LLMs.

papers.title: Dense Passage Retrieval for Open-Domain Question Answering
papers.abstract: Introduces DPR for dense retrieval outperforming sparse methods like BM25.

papers.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
papers.abstract: Combines retrieval with generation for knowledge-grounded language models.

papers.title: Zephyr: Direct Distillation of LM Alignment
papers.abstract: Distilled DPO approach for aligning small LMs without human feedback.

papers.title: Lost in the Middle: How Language Models Use Long Contexts
papers.abstract: Reveals U-shaped attention pattern where LLMs struggle with middle context.

papers.title: Mastering the game of Go with deep neural networks and tree search
papers.abstract: AlphaGo: First program to defeat a professional Go player.

papers.title: Better & Faster Large Language Models via Multi-token Prediction
papers.abstract: Training LLMs to predict multiple tokens simultaneously for efficiency.
